compiled_state.title: As a developer, I am blocked from committing tests with common anti-patterns by a pre-commit quality gate
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a configurable pre-commit test quality gate that uses language-specific bloat detection tools with Semgrep custom rules as the language-agnostic fallback. The gate detects common anti-patterns in new or modified test files before they reach review.
**Why**: A deterministic static analysis gate catches obvious test anti-patterns earlier and faster than LLM-based review, reducing review overhead and providing immediate developer feedback.
**Scope**:
- IN: Pre-commit hook that runs on new or modified test files only (diff-based)
- IN: Semgrep custom rules targeting: open/cat/grep of source files in test assertions, os.path.exists/Path.exists as sole assertion, ast.parse/inspect.getsource in tests
- IN: Language-specific tool support (PyNose for Python, eslint-plugin-jest for JS/TS) configured via dso-config.conf
- IN: Calibration for high confidence only — gate blocks only when anti-pattern detection confidence is high
- IN: Timeout budget to stay under ~73s tool timeout ceiling
- IN: Graceful degradation when tools are not installed (gate disables rather than blocks)
- OUT: Review pipeline overlay (separate story)
- OUT: Onboarding/installation instructions (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a pre-commit gate detects common test anti-patterns in new or modified test files using configurable language-specific tools with Semgrep as fallback
  ← Satisfies: "A configurable pre-commit test quality gate uses language-specific bloat detection tools with Semgrep custom rules as the language-agnostic fallback"
- When this story is complete, the active detection tool is configured via dso-config.conf
  ← Satisfies: "the active tool is configured via dso-config.conf"
- When this story is complete, the gate only blocks commits when there is high confidence an anti-pattern has been detected
  ← Satisfies: "the gate is calibrated to avoid false positives"
- When this story is complete, the gate degrades gracefully when tools are not installed
  ← Satisfies: "graceful degradation when installation fails — gate disables rather than blocks"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD done-of-done

## Considerations

- [Testing] Semgrep custom rules need calibration to avoid false positives — eval coverage for edge cases
- [Reliability] Pre-commit gate must respect ~73s tool timeout ceiling — enforce timeout budget
- [Reliability] Graceful degradation when Semgrep or language-specific tools are not installed

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability] The ~73s timeout ceiling is shared across ALL pre-commit hooks. The new hook must be registered with explicit timeout via pre-commit-wrapper.sh. Measure baseline hook chain duration before setting timeout budget.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Consideration: [Integration] New pre-commit hook must be registered in: .pre-commit-config.yaml, reinstall-hooks.sh, dso-setup.sh, and .hook-drift-allowlist if applicable.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Story complete: all 4 tasks closed, completion-verifier PASS on all 5 done definitions.
comments.author: Test
